source.description: Six-panel generated raster process sheet cropped into five step frames and one finished pencil sketch.
source.notes: Created for the 2026-05-24 honest Sketcha.day archive backfill.

process_strategy: One diagonal clothespin is constructed from two long wooden halves, then notches, spring wire, wood grain, and a cast shadow are added before final line and tone reinforcement.

final_elements.name: diagonal clothespin body
final_elements.description: A long narrow clothespin silhouette set on a slight diagonal.

final_elements.name: two wooden halves
final_elements.description: Parallel upper and lower wooden jaws separated by a central gap.

final_elements.name: pinched jaw tips
final_elements.description: Tapered gripping ends at the front of the clothespin.

final_elements.name: center spring coil
final_elements.description: A small metal coil near the middle of the clothespin.

final_elements.name: wire arms
final_elements.description: Curved wire arms crossing from the spring into the wooden halves.

final_elements.name: side notches
final_elements.description: Small carved notches beside the spring area.

final_elements.name: wood grain lines
final_elements.description: Light grain strokes following the length of the wooden halves.

final_elements.name: warm wood shading
final_elements.description: Restrained tan pencil tone on the already drawn wood.

final_elements.name: cast shadow
final_elements.description: A soft shadow beneath the diagonal pin.

frames.visible_job: Places the diagonal clothespin body as one simple tapered guide shape.
Divides the guide into two wooden halves and shapes the pinched jaw tips.
Adds the center spring coil and wire arms without changing the wooden body.
Adds small side notches around the already placed spring area.
Adds wood grain, warm wood shading, and a soft cast shadow to the existing clothespin.

final_step.visible_job: Only clarifies the existing edges, spring, grain, warm tone, and shadow.

rejection_checks: No new jaw, spring, wire arm, notch, grain field, or shadow should first appear in the final image.
The clothespin must stay diagonal with the same taper and spring position in every frame.
The final must look like a small pencil sketch, not a clean vector icon.
No adjacent frame should look unchanged at card size.
